major: Latin American & Latino Studies/Sociology Combined B.A.

courses.title: Introduction to Latin American and Latino Studies
Issues and Problems in American Society

notes: All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website .

This is a non-screening major: completion of specific major coursework is not required for admission. However, students are strongly encouraged to complete as many lower-division courses as possible from the " Recommended Preparation " section below to support timely degree completion. Students applying to non-screening majors are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements prior to transfer. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor.

The Latin American and Latino Studies (LALS) and Sociology (SOCY) programs at UC Santa Cruz welcome transfer students. Prospective transfer students interested in a combined LALS and SOCY major are encouraged to complete three lower-division courses, or their articulated equivalents, prior to transferring: One LALS introductory course Two SOCY introductory courses Please visit the Latin American and Latino Studies (LALS) website to learn more about this major. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Latin American and Latino Studies/Sociology Combined B.A. Program Statement Prospective transfer students who would like to connect with an LALS advisor are strongly encouraged to email lalsadvising@ucsc.edu .
